judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 satisfies all four expectations (F-11 stays concurrent in the early wave, and the shared RESULTS_DB collision is flagged with a concrete override fix), while Report 2 misses (b) by deferring F-11 to a later test-only wave and misses (d) by downplaying the RESULTS_DB path as an unused minor detail rather than a concurrency hazard.

report_1.evidence: Group B — registry/config (pipeline/registry.py): F-02 add aurora-1 endpoint, F-05 bump default to rubric-v3, F-09 repoint DEFAULT_DATASET to fixtures/eval_set.jsonl. Three edits, one file → one owner.

report_2.evidence: pipeline/registry.py | F-02, F-05, F-09 (three constant edits in a 12-line file) ... Engineer A → pipeline/registry.py: F-05, F-09 (trivial), plus F-02 once the endpoint URL is known.

report_1.evidence: Groups A/B/C/D touch fully disjoint files and run as one parallel wave (Wave 1). Inside a group, order the fix before its test (A: F-04 → F-12; C: F-01 → F-11). F-06 is a deferred Wave 2, done by a single owner on score.py only after the threshold decision returns.

report_2.evidence: Wave 2 — Tests and policy, after Wave 1 lands (depend on it): F-11 written by Engineer C after F-01, so it asserts the chosen multi-line-join semantics.

report_1.evidence: F-06 (tighten PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS) — escalate, don't assign. It's a user-facing behavior change (every borderline answer scores lower) and it's underspecified... get the exact thresholds and sign-off, then implement in Wave 2. Don't let an engineer guess the numbers.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — not a defect; a policy decision. "Too lenient… tighten them" is subjective and gives no target values. Changing PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS silently re-scores historical/borderline outputs and needs an eval-owner sign-off plus re-baselining. → Escalate for a decision, don't hand out as a mechanical fix.

report_1.evidence: A per-engineer RESULTS_DB. conftest.py defaults to the shared /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db; each engineer should export their own RESULTS_DB path (and ensure the dir exists/writable) so parallel suites don't collide.

report_2.evidence: Minor: tests/conftest.py reads RESULTS_DB (default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). No current test uses it, but anyone writing tests that touch it needs that path or the env var set.
